function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public internet for information. Each result will contain a url, a title, and one excerpt taken directly from the page.

start_date.description: (optional) The start date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Must not be equal to end_date.

end_date.description: (optional) The end date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. If the value is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

number_of_results.description: (optional) The number of search results to return.

function.name: retrieve_information
function.description: Retrieves critical data segments from a previously ingested document via hybrid lexical and semantic calculations. Crucial Requirement: The search prompt argument MUST contain the token placeholder containing your storage key inside double curly brackets, exactly format formatted as: {{key_name}} alongside your query description text.

prompt.description: Your target question containing the explicit target reference mapping (e.g., 'What were the total research costs in {{my_document_key}}?').

preserve_document_order.description: Set to True if processing complex structural data dependencies across sequential disclosures.

function.name: parse_html_page
function.description: Parses an HTML document or SEC filing. It automatically extracts key metadata, converts asset tables to clean markdown, slices sections into optimal word chunks, and runs concurrent contextual LLM enrichment before updating the disk cache.

url.description: The target URL of the HTML page/filing to process.

key.description: The unique cache storage key for saving and loading the index.

function.name: edgar_search
function.description: Search the EDGAR Database through the SEC API. You should provide a search query. You can also optionally provide a start date, an end date, a page number, top N results, a list of form types, and/or a list of CIKs. The results are returned as a list of dictionaries, each containing the metadata for a filing. It does not contain the full text of the filing.

search_query.description: The case-insensitive search-term or phrase to search the contents of filings and their attachments. This can be a single word, phrase, or combination of words and phrases. Supported search features include wildcards (*), Boolean operators (OR, NOT), and exact phrase matching by enclosing phrases in quotation marks ("exact phrase"). By default, all terms are joined by an implicit AND operator.

form_types.description: (optional) Limits search to specific EDGAR form types (e.g., ['8-K', '10-Q']) list of strings. Default: all form types

ciks.description: (optional) Filters results to filings from specified CIKs, type list of strings. Leading zeros are optional but may be included. Example: [ "0001811414", "1318605" ]. Default: all CIKs

start_date.description: (optional) Start date for the search range in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

end_date.description: (optional) End date for the search range, in the same format as startDate. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

page.description: (optional) Used for pagination. Each page contains up to 100 matching filings. Increase the page number to retrieve the next set of 100 filings. Example: 3 retrieves the third page. Default: 1

top_n_results.description: (optional) Return only the first N results out of 100 from the page. If not provided, all 100 results will be returned. E.g. if page is 2, and top_n_results is 10, you will receive results 100 to 110.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the result. Use this tool for all arithmetic calculations instead of computing by hand. Supports: +, -, *, /, ** (exponentiation), % (modulo), and parentheses for grouping. Available functions: abs(), min(), max(), sqrt(), log(), log10(). Examples: '(5000 - 3200) * 0.21', '(2865507 / 1905871) ** 0.5 - 1', '14060 / 2148'.

function.name: price_history
function.description: Fetch historical daily price data for a specific asset class. Returns a CSV table with one row per day. Use asset_class='equity' or 'etf' for US-listed stocks/ETFs (e.g. AAPL, SPY), 'crypto' for pairs like btcusd (lowercase, no dash), or 'fx' for pairs like audusd. Non-US equities and most indices/futures are not covered by the pricing provider. Each row includes raw OHLC (open/high/low/close), split- and dividend-adjusted OHLC (adjOpen/adjHigh/adjLow/adjClose), raw and adjusted volume, divCash (dividend amount on the date), and splitFactor.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol. High-level symbology rules: (1) Equity/ETF/mutual fund: use the US exchange-listed symbol (AAPL, SPY, VFIAX). Symbols are case-insensitive and contain only letters and dashes. (2) Share classes use a dash, not a dot: 'BRK-A' / 'BRK-B' (not 'BRK.A'), 'BF-B'. (3) Securities are identified by the ticker as listed on the primary US exchange; non-US listings, OTC pink sheets, indices, and futures are generally not covered. (4) Tickers can be reused after delisting; data is point-in-time for the entity that held the ticker on each date. (5) Crypto: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'btcusd', 'ethusd'. (6) FX: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'audusd', 'eurusd'.

asset_class.description: Asset class used to route to the proper pricing endpoint.

function.name: submit_final_result
function.description: Submits the final answer to the user. You should include your final answer, as well as any necessary reasoning, justification, calculations, and explanation. Finally, you should provide any sources used to answer the question. You MUST use this tool to submit your final result. The user will not see your response if you do not use this tool to submit. You will not be able to continue working after this tool is called; the conversation will be ended.

final_result.description: The final result to submit to the user

steps.message:
You are an IPO and financial diligence agent.

You are given a question and must answer it using the tools provided.

You cannot interact with the user or ask clarifying questions.

Answer solely based on available information and tools.

Assume the current date is May 31, 2026.

---

TOOLS AND DATA SOURCES

You have access to a document storage system.

You may store parsed HTML retrieved from the web and later retrieve
information using LLM-based retrieval prompts.

Use this system to avoid context-window limitations.

When working with long filings, prefer section-specific retrieval prompts
rather than broad document-level retrieval.

Examples:

- retrieve the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table from MD&A
- retrieve the glossary definition of Starshield
- retrieve the controlled-company governance disclosure

For peer-comparison questions:

- retrieve peer SEC filings using edgar_search
- do not rely on memory for peer financial figures

For historical public-equity price data not available in SEC filings:

- use price_history as primary source
- use raw unadjusted prices unless adjusted prices are requested
- fall back to web_search only if price_history fails

Stock indices are not covered by price_history.

Use authoritative sources such as FRED or web_search for index levels.

---

SOURCE HIERARCHY

Prioritize sources in this order:

1. S-1 / S-1A filings
2. Incorporated SEC filings
3. SEC exhibits and merger documents
4. SEC periodic filings
5. Company earnings materials
6. External sources

If figures differ across sources:

prefer the most authoritative SEC source containing the directly reported
figure and explain material discrepancies when relevant.

SEC filings are the primary and most authoritative source of financial and
disclosure information.

---

ANSWER STANDARDS

You must call submit_final_result with the completed answer.

Your submission will not be processed unless submit_final_result is called.

Include:

- complete reasoning
- calculations
- supporting analysis

You will be evaluated on:

- final-answer accuracy
- analytical correctness

Do not round intermediate calculations.

Round only final reported values.

When possible, report calculated figures to at least two decimal places.

Report financial figures using the same scale and units used in the filing
unless otherwise specified.

Share prices should be reported with two decimal places.

---

DISCLOSURE DISCIPLINE

Before reaching a conclusion:

determine whether the filing provides sufficient information to answer:

- directly
- partially
- only through inference

Distinguish carefully among:

- facts explicitly disclosed
- reasonable filing-supported inferences
- information not disclosed

Do not speculate beyond what the filing supports.

If material information is absent:

explicitly state that the filing does not provide sufficient information to
conclude.

Absence of disclosure may itself be analytically material.

---

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Treat separately:

- historical facts
- roadmap targets
- commercialization expectations
- management intentions
- forward-looking statements

Do not present aspirations or plans as historical outcomes.

---

GOVERNANCE AND LEGAL QUESTIONS

For governance, legal, or disclosure-language questions:

use the filing's precise terminology whenever possible.

Distinguish among:

- requirements
- exemptions
- company elections
- forward-looking language

---

ACCOUNTING POLICY QUESTIONS

For accounting-policy questions:

1. Identify the relevant disclosed accounting policy or treatment
2. Determine whether the filing explicitly resolves the issue
3. If unresolved, state that the filing does not specify treatment

---

NON-GAAP QUESTIONS

For non-GAAP measures:

identify:

- the starting GAAP metric
- each disclosed adjustment
- whether economically recurring costs are excluded

Do not accept non-GAAP measures without analyzing their construction.

---

AUDIENCE TAGS

Questions may include audience tags such as:

[PUBLIC | BANK]
[VC | CREDIT]
[LEGAL | ACCOUNTING]

Use these tags to calibrate terminology and analytical emphasis while
remaining grounded in disclosed facts.

---

SOURCES

At the end of your answer provide:

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "...",
      "name": "..."
    }
  ]
}


---


FILING CONTEXT

The primary filing for this benchmark is the SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm

Important:

The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under
common-control accounting to include:

- xAI (merged February 2, 2026)
- X Holdings (merged into xAI March 28, 2025)

for all historical periods presented.

No period reflects standalone SpaceX operations exclusively.

When interpreting historical segment figures, state whether they reflect the
recast consolidated entity.

SpaceX (SPCX) has no public trading history as of May 30, 2026.

Do not attempt price_history retrieval for SpaceX or SPCX.

For peer valuation or peer financial benchmarking, retrieve peer-company SEC
filings using edgar_search rather than relying on memory.

Question:
Estimate implied monthly Starlink ARPU using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and disclosed subscriber counts. State all assumptions and identify what the filing’s subscriber and revenue definitions exclude from this ARPU estimate.

steps.reasoning_content:
The user is asking me to estimate implied monthly Starlink ARPU (Average Revenue Per User) using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and disclosed subscriber counts from the SpaceX S-1 filing. I need to:

1. First, parse the SpaceX S-1 filing to find:
   - Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue (segment-level or service-level)
   - Starlink subscriber counts (disclosed in the filing)
   - Definitions of what's included/excluded in each

2. Calculate the implied monthly ARPU
3. State assumptions and exclusions

Let me start by parsing the S-1 filing.

results.content: SUCCESS: Loaded portable assets for key: spacex_s1

steps.reasoning_content: Good, the filing is loaded. Now let me search for Starlink subscriber counts and Connectivity segment revenue.

arguments.prompt: What were the Starlink subscriber counts disclosed in {{spacex_s1}}? Look for total subscribers, residential subscribers, business subscribers, or any subscriber count metrics. Include the specific numbers and the dates/periods they refer to.

What was the Connectivity segment revenue for Q1 2026 and other periods in {{spacex_s1}}? Look for segment-level revenue breakdowns including Connectivity revenue figures, and note whether they are quarterly or annual. Also look for any Starlink-specific revenue figures.

results.content:
# Starlink Subscriber Counts

Based on the document chunks provided, here are the Starlink subscriber metrics disclosed:

## Primary Subscriber Metrics

| Period | Starlink Subscribers (millions) | Starlink Subscriber ARPU |
|--------|-------------------------------|--------------------------|
| **Q1 2026** (Three Months Ended March 31, 2026) | **10.3** | $66/month |
| **Q1 2025** (Three Months Ended March 31, 2025) | **5.0** | $86/month |
| **FY 2025** (Year Ended December 31, 2025) | **8.9** | $81/month |
| **FY 2024** (Year Ended December 31, 2024) | **4.4** | $91/month |
| **FY 2023** (Year Ended December 31, 2023) | **2.3** | $99/month |

## Growth Rates
- **Q1 2026 vs Q1 2025:** 105% growth (5.0M → 10.3M)
- **FY 2025 vs FY 2024:** 99.9% growth (4.4M → 8.9M)
- **FY 2024 vs FY 2023:** 96.5% growth (2.3M → 4.4M)

## Important Definitions & Context

- **Starlink Subscribers** are defined as unique **Service Lines** — not individual users. The document explicitly notes that "the number of individual end users who access Starlink is already likely **meaningfully higher than 10.3 million**," as multiple people may share a single Service Line.
- This metric includes both **Personal** (Residential, Roam) and **Business** (Local Priority, Global Priority) subscription plans.
- It **excludes** managed enterprise and government customers in aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed sites, and government entities.
- Subscribers are counted across **164 countries, territories, and other markets**.

## Additional Metric
- **Satellite-to-mobile:** approximately **7.4 million monthly unique devices** across approximately 30 countries (as of March 31, 2026) — this is a separate metric from Starlink Subscribers.

---

**Note:** The document does **not** break out subscribers into separate residential vs. business subscriber counts. ARPU has been deliberately declining (from $99/month in 2023 to $66/month in Q1 2026) as the company prioritizes subscriber growth and international expansion over pricing.

Based on the provided financial excerpts, here are the **Connectivity segment revenue figures** for the periods mentioned:

### Quarterly Revenue (Three Months Ended March 31)
- **Q1 2026:** $3,257 million  
- **Q1 2025:** $2,475 million  

This represents a **year-over-year increase of $782 million (31.6%)** in Q1 2026, driven primarily by Starlink subscriber growth and enterprise sales.

### Annual Revenue (Year Ended December 31)
- **2025:** $11,387 million  
- **2024:** $7,599 million  
- **2023:** $3,869 million  

The 2025 figure shows a **49.8% year-over-year increase** compared to 2024, driven by strong growth in both consumer (Starlink) and enterprise/government segments.

### Key Context from the MD&A
- The Connectivity segment’s revenue growth is attributed to:
  - **Consumer subscribers:** Starlink subscriber base grew by 104.7% YoY in Q1 2026, though average revenue per user (ARPU) declined 22.9% due to international expansion and lower-priced plans.
  - **Enterprise & Government:** Aviation, maritime, and mobile connectivity demand increased, though government sales saw a partial offset.
- For the full year 2025, consumer subscriber revenue grew by $2,377 million, and enterprise/government revenue increased by $1,411 million.

### Summary Table
| Period | Connectivity Segment Revenue | Year-over-Year Change |
|--------|-------------------------------|------------------------|
| Q1 2026 | $3,257 million | +31.6% vs. Q1 2025 |
| Q1 2025 | $2,475 million | — |
| FY 2025 | $11,387 million | +49.8% vs. FY 2024 |
| FY 2024 | $7,599 million | +96.4% vs. FY 2023 |
| FY 2023 | $3,869 million | — |

These figures highlight the Connectivity segment’s rapid scaling, driven primarily by Starlink’s global expansion and increasing enterprise adoption.

steps.reasoning_content:
Interesting - so the filing actually discloses both:
1. Connectivity segment revenue (which includes both consumer/Starlink AND enterprise/government)
2. Starlink Subscriber ARPU directly ($66/month for Q1 2026)

The filing itself already provides the ARPU figure. But the user asks me to "estimate implied monthly Starlink ARPU using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and disclosed subscriber counts." This suggests they want me to calculate it myself using the raw data.

Let me get more detail on what the Connectivity revenue includes and what's excluded from the subscriber count definition, so I can properly note assumptions and exclusions.

Let me also look for any breakdown of Connectivity revenue between consumer (Starlink) and enterprise/government.

arguments.prompt: In {{spacex_s1}}, is Connectivity segment revenue broken down between consumer Starlink revenue and enterprise/government revenue? Look for any sub-segments or revenue breakdowns within the Connectivity segment. Also, what exactly is included in the Connectivity segment - define its components.

In {{spacex_s1}}, look for the definition of Starlink Subscriber ARPU - how is it calculated? What is the numerator and denominator? Is it based on Connectivity segment revenue or a subset? What is excluded from the subscriber count (enterprise, government, managed services)?

results.content:
Based on the provided financial disclosures, here is a breakdown of the Connectivity segment's revenue and a definition of its components.

### Connectivity Segment Revenue Breakdown (Consumer vs. Enterprise & Government)

Yes, the Connectivity segment's revenue is broken down between **Consumer Starlink revenue** and **Enterprise & Government revenue**. The primary source for this breakdown is the disaggregated revenue table.

For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, the split was as follows:

| Revenue Line Item | Amount (2025) | Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Consumer** | $7,208 million | Revenue from individual and residential Starlink subscribers. |
| **Enterprise & Government** | $4,179 million | Revenue from business and public-sector customers, **which includes Starlink Mobile service offerings.** |
| **Total Connectivity** | **$11,387 million** | Sum of Consumer and Enterprise & Government revenue. |

**Growth Drivers (as per MD&A):**
*   **Consumer revenue** growth is driven by increases in the number of Starlink subscribers, partially offset by a declining Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) due to international expansion and lower-priced plans.
*   **Enterprise & Government revenue** growth is driven by the expansion of aviation, maritime, mobility, and other enterprise businesses, as well as government connectivity contracts (Starshield).

---

### Definition: Components of the Connectivity Segment

The Connectivity segment encompasses the **Starlink business**, generating revenue from two primary sources: **services** and **products**.

**1. Services:**
*   **Consumer Broadband:** Monthly subscription fees for high-speed internet access, priced by geography and speed. Revenue is recognized ratably over the subscription period (month-to-month).
*   **Enterprise & Government Connectivity:** A mix of contract structures:
    *   **Subscriptions & Data Usage:** Similar to consumer plans but often with customized terms and SLAs.
    *   **Capacity Contracts:** Agreements for dedicated network capacity.
    *   **Starshield:** Long-term, multi-year contracts for a secure government satellite network. Revenue for these is recognized **over time using the cost-to-cost input method** (percentage-of-completion).
*   **Starlink Mobile:** Revenue from satellite-to-mobile services (texting, voice, data) provided through **revenue-sharing arrangements with Mobile Network Operator (MNO) partners**.

**2. Products:**
*   **Starlink Kit Sales:** One-time revenue from the sale of hardware kits (including the satellite terminal). Revenue is recognized **upon delivery to the customer** (or upon installation for certain enterprise customers), net of returns and allowances. The disclosure notes state, "All of products revenue is attributable to the Connectivity segment."

**In summary,** the Connectivity segment is the company's **satellite-based internet service provider (Starlink)**, offering broadband and mobile connectivity services to consumers, businesses, and governments worldwide, along with the associated hardware kits needed to access the service.

Based on the provided S-1 preamble sections, the definition and calculation of **Starlink Subscriber ARPU** are as follows:

### Calculation Formula:
**Starlink Subscriber ARPU** = (Service revenue from Starlink Subscribers for the period) ÷ (Average number of Starlink Subscribers during the period) ÷ (Number of months in the period)

### Key Details:

1.  **Numerator**: Service revenue generated specifically from **Starlink Subscribers**.
2.  **Denominator**: The average number of Starlink Subscribers during the period, multiplied by the number of months in the period.

### What is Excluded from the "Starlink Subscribers" Count (and thus from the ARPU calculation's denominator):
*   **Managed enterprise and government customers** with direct, negotiated agreements with the Starlink sales team. This includes contracts in domains such as:
    *   Aviation
    *   Maritime
    *   Land mobility
    *   Fixed sites
    *   Government entities (covered by the **Starshield** product)

### What is Included in the "Starlink Subscribers" Count:
*   Unique **Service Lines** directly assigned to a consumer-facing Starlink.com account.
*   Plans include both **Personal** (e.g., Residential, Roam) and **Business** (e.g., Local Priority, Global Priority) subscriptions.
*   The count is based on Service Lines at the end of the period, not unique devices or persons (as one household/business can have multiple Service Lines).

### Strategic Context:
The company explicitly states that its strategy is focused on **scale and revenue growth over ARPU growth**. They expect Starlink Subscriber ARPU to continue declining due to:
*   International expansion into markets with lower purchasing power.
*   The addition of lower-priced service plans.
*   Adjustments to monthly fees for broadband offerings.
This decline is seen as offset by increased scale and cost efficiencies, driving overall revenue growth.

steps.reasoning_content:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me summarize what I know:

**Q1 2026 Connectivity Segment Revenue:** $3,257 million

**Starlink Subscribers (Q1 2026):** 10.3 million (end of period)
- Q1 2025 subscribers: 5.0 million
- Average subscribers for Q1 2026 would be approximately (5.0 + 10.3) / 2 = 7.65 million (if we use beginning and end of period averages)

Wait, actually the disclosure says Q1 2026 subscriber count is 10.3 million and Q1 2025 is 5.0 million. But we need the average for the period.

Actually, the filing already discloses the ARPU directly:
- Q1 2026: $66/month
- Q1 2025: $86/month

But the user asks me to estimate the implied monthly ARPU using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and subscriber counts.

The issue is: Connectivity revenue ≠ Starlink Subscriber revenue. Connectivity revenue includes:
1. Consumer Starlink subscriber revenue
2. Enterprise & Government revenue (aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed sites, government/Starshield)
3. Product revenue (Starlink kit sales)

So if I divide total Connectivity revenue by subscribers, I'd get a HIGHER ARPU than the disclosed $66/month because:
- The numerator would include enterprise/government revenue and product sales
- The denominator only includes consumer/business self-service subscribers

Let me calculate both:
1. A "blended" ARPU using total Connectivity revenue / subscribers
2. Note that the actual Starlink Subscriber ARPU is different

For Q1 2026:
- Connectivity revenue: $3,257 million
- Starlink Subscribers: 10.3 million (end of period)

But wait - we need average subscribers for the quarter. Let me think about what average subscriber count would be reasonable.

Actually, the filing discloses:
- Q4 2025 (end of year): 8.9 million subscribers
- Q1 2026 (end of period): 10.3 million subscribers

So the average for Q1 2026 would be approximately (8.9 + 10.3) / 2 = 9.6 million

Or maybe they use beginning of period? Let me think about this more carefully.

Actually, the user asked me to estimate implied monthly ARPU using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and subscriber counts. Let me do both:
1. Simple calculation: Total Connectivity revenue / (average subscribers × 3 months)
2. Note the difference from the disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU

Let me also check if there's quarterly data available to estimate average subscribers more precisely.

Actually, let me also check what FY2025 annual figures look like:
- FY2025 Connectivity revenue: $11,387 million
- FY2025 Starlink ARPU: $81/month
- FY2025 subscribers: 8.9 million (end of period)

If I back into it: $81/month × 12 months = $972/year per subscriber
If average subscribers during 2025 were, say, ~6.65 million (midpoint of 4.4M end-2024 and 8.9M end-2025):
Revenue = $972 × 6.65M = ~$6.46 billion

But total Connectivity was $11,387 million. So Consumer Starlink revenue was $7,208 million (as disclosed for 2025). That means:
$7,208M / (6.65M × 12) = ~$90.3/month

Hmm, that's higher than $81. So the average subscriber calculation might be different, or there's something else going on.

Actually, wait - the $81 ARPU is explicitly disclosed. Let me just compute what the "implied" ARPU would be if you naively used total Connectivity revenue.

For Q1 2026:
- Total Connectivity revenue: $3,257 million
- Average subscribers: approximately (8.9 + 10.3) / 2 = 9.6 million
- Implied monthly ARPU = $3,257M / (9.6M × 3) = $3,257M / 28.8M = ~$113.09/month

That's significantly higher than the disclosed $66/month because Connectivity revenue includes enterprise/government and product revenue.

The actual disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU of $66/month is based on:
- Numerator: Service revenue from Starlink Subscribers only (excludes enterprise/government, product sales)
- Denominator: Average Starlink Subscribers × 3 months

Let me verify this with Q1 2025:
- Q1 2025 Connectivity revenue: $2,475 million
- Q1 2025 subscribers: 5.0 million (end of period)
- FY2024 subscribers: 4.4 million (end of period)
- Average Q1 2025 subscribers: approximately (4.4 + 5.0) / 2 = 4.7 million
- Implied monthly ARPU = $2,475M / (4.7M × 3) = $2,475M / 14.1M = ~$175.53/month

That's much higher than $86/month, confirming that Connectivity revenue includes much more than just Starlink subscriber service revenue.

So my answer should:

1. Calculate the "naive" implied ARPU using total Connectivity revenue / average subscribers
2. Explain why this overstates true Starlink Subscriber ARPU
3. Note the disclosed ARPU figure ($66/month) and how it's computed differently
4. List all the exclusions from the subscriber count and from the ARPU numerator

Let me also think about whether the user wants me to use the beginning, end, or average subscriber count. The standard practice for ARPU calculations is to use average subscribers during the period.

Let me compute both scenarios (end-of-period and average):

**Using end-of-period subscribers (10.3M):**
Implied ARPU = $3,257M / (10.3M × 3) = $3,257M / 30.9M = ~$105.40/month

**Using average subscribers (9.6M):**
Implied ARPU = $3,257M / (9.6M × 3) = $3,257M / 28.8M = ~$113.09/month

I should present both and explain the differences.

Actually, I realize the filing may also provide FY2025 end subscriber count which would be the Q1 2026 beginning count. Let me check - the filing says FY2025 subscribers were 8.9 million.

So:
- Beginning of Q1 2026 (end of FY2025): 8.9 million
- End of Q1 2026: 10.3 million
- Average for Q1 2026: (8.9 + 10.3) / 2 = 9.6 million

Let me do the calculations properly.

arguments.prompt: In {{spacex_s1}}, what was the Starlink subscriber count at the end of FY2025 (December 31, 2025) and the end of Q1 2026 (March 31, 2026)? Also, is there any disclosure about how average subscribers are calculated for the ARPU metric - do they use beginning of period, end of period, or a daily/monthly average? Also look for quarterly breakdowns of Connectivity revenue for all four quarters of 2025 if available.

In {{spacex_s1}}, look for product revenue within the Connectivity segment for Q1 2026 and Q1 2025. Is product revenue (Starlink kit sales) broken out separately from service revenue within the Connectivity segment? Also look for any quarterly disaggregated revenue tables showing consumer vs enterprise breakdown by quarter.

results.content:
Based on the provided documents, here are the answers to your questions:

### 1. Starlink Subscriber Count
*   **At the end of FY2025 (December 31, 2025):** **8.9 million subscribers.**
    *   *Source:* From the unaudited segment data table, the "Year Ended December 31, 2025" column shows Starlink Subscribers at 8.9 million.
*   **At the end of Q1 2026 (March 31, 2026):** **10.3 million subscribers.**
    *   *Source:* This figure is stated multiple times in the Business section preamble and the Q1 2026 financial data.

### 2. Calculation Methodology for ARPU
Yes, the method is disclosed.
*   **Calculation:** "We calculate ARPU as service revenue generated from Starlink Subscribers during the period divided by (i) the **average number of Starlink Subscribers during the period** and by (ii) the number of months in the period."
*   This indicates they use an **average for the period**, not a point-in-time figure (like beginning or end of period). The standard accounting method is typically a weighted average over the months in the quarter/year.

### 3. Quarterly Breakdown of Connectivity Revenue for 2025
The provided documents **do not include a quarterly breakdown of Connectivity revenue for all four quarters of 2025.**
*   The financial tables only provide **annual** Connectivity revenue for 2025 ($11,387 million) and **quarterly** data for Q1 2026 ($3,257 million) and Q1 2025 ($2,475 million).
*   The year-over-year growth analysis for 2025 references annual figures only.

Based on the provided documents:

**1. Product Revenue Breakout within Connectivity Segment:**
Yes, **product revenue is broken out separately from service revenue within the Connectivity segment.**

The first disaggregated revenue table in the Preamble explicitly shows this for the quarterly periods:
- **Three Months Ended March 31, 2026:** Products = $380 million
- **Three Months Ended March 31, 2025:** Products = $352 million

The narrative directly below this table confirms: "**All of products revenue is attributable to the Connectivity segment.**" This product revenue represents sales of the Starlink Kit (inclusive of the terminal).

**2. Quarterly Consumer vs. Enterprise Breakdown by Quarter:**
**No, a specific quarterly table breaking down Connectivity revenue into "Consumer" vs. "Enterprise & Government" is not provided in the excerpts.**

The detailed breakdown of Connectivity revenue into Consumer and Enterprise & Government is only provided in **annual** disaggregated tables (for years ended December 31, 2025, 2024, and 2023). The narrative sections of the MD&A do provide *quarterly year-over-year growth figures* for these sub-categories, but they are not presented in a comparative quarterly table.

For example, the MD&A states for Q1 2026 vs. Q1 2025:
*   **Consumer revenue** increased by $656 million, driven by 104.7% growth in Starlink Subscribers, offset by a 22.9% decline in ARPU.
*   **Enterprise and government revenue** increased by $126 million, with growth in aviation, maritime, and mobile connectivity partially offset by a decrease in government connectivity.

**Summary:**
*   **Product (Kit) revenue** is clearly and separately reported within the Connectivity segment for each quarter.
*   A **quarterly tabular breakdown** of Connectivity revenue into Consumer vs. Enterprise & Government is **not** present; this detail is only available for full fiscal years. The quarterly movement is described only in the MD&A narrative.

steps.message: Now I have all the data needed. Let me compute the implied ARPU calculations.
steps.reasoning_content:
Now I have much better information. Let me compute the implied ARPU in different ways:

**Q1 2026 Data:**
- Total Connectivity Revenue: $3,257 million
- Products Revenue (Starlink Kit Sales): $380 million
- Connectivity Service Revenue: $3,257M - $380M = $2,877 million
- Starlink Subscribers (end of period): 10.3 million
- Starlink Subscribers (beginning of period = FY2025 end): 8.9 million
- Average Starlink Subscribers: (8.9 + 10.3) / 2 = 9.6 million

**Q1 2025 Data:**
- Total Connectivity Revenue: $2,475 million
- Products Revenue: $352 million
- Connectivity Service Revenue: $2,475M - $352M = $2,123 million
- Starlink Subscribers (end of period): 5.0 million
- Starlink Subscribers (beginning of period = FY2024 end): 4.4 million
- Average Starlink Subscribers: (4.4 + 5.0) / 2 = 4.7 million

Now let me compute various implied ARPUs:

**Scenario 1: Total Connectivity Revenue / (Average Subscribers × 3)**
Q1 2026: $3,257M / (9.6M × 3) = $3,257M / 28.8M = $113.09/month
Q1 2025: $2,475M / (4.7M × 3) = $2,475M / 14.1M = $175.53/month

**Scenario 2: Connectivity Service Revenue / (Average Subscribers × 3) - excluding products**
Q1 2026: $2,877M / (9.6M × 3) = $2,877M / 28.8M = $99.90/month
Q1 2025: $2,123M / (4.7M × 3) = $2,123M / 14.1M = $150.57/month

**Scenario 3: Using end-of-period subscribers instead of average**
Q1 2026 Total: $3,257M / (10.3M × 3) = $3,257M / 30.9M = $105.40/month
Q1 2026 Service: $2,877M / (10.3M × 3) = $2,877M / 30.9M = $93.11/month

**Disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU:** $66/month for Q1 2026

So the difference between my implied service revenue ARPU ($99.90/month) and the disclosed $66/month is because Connectivity service revenue includes enterprise/government revenue (aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed sites, Starshield) that is excluded from the Starlink Subscriber ARPU calculation.

Using the Q1 2025 comparison to back into what portion of revenue goes to Starlink subscribers:
- Disclosed Q1 2025 ARPU: $86/month
- Average Q1 2025 subscribers: 4.7 million
- Starlink Subscriber Service Revenue = $86 × 4.7M × 3 = $1,211.4 million
- Total Q1 2025 Connectivity Service Revenue: $2,123 million
- Enterprise/Other Service Revenue: $2,123M - $1,211.4M = $911.6 million

For Q1 2026:
- Disclosed ARPU: $66/month
- Average subscribers: 9.6 million
- Starlink Subscriber Service Revenue = $66 × 9.6M × 3 = $1,900.8 million
- Total Q1 2026 Connectivity Service Revenue: $2,877 million
- Enterprise/Other Service Revenue: $2,877M - $1,900.8M = $976.2 million

This makes sense - enterprise revenue grew but not as fast as consumer revenue in absolute terms.

Actually, let me verify this with the annual FY2025 data:
- FY2025 ARPU: $81/month
- FY2025 end subscribers: 8.9 million
- Beginning FY2025 (end FY2024): 4.4 million
- Average FY2025 subscribers: (4.4 + 8.9) / 2 = 6.65 million
- Starlink Subscriber Service Revenue = $81 × 6.65M × 12 = $6,475.8 million
- Total Connectivity Revenue FY2025: $11,387 million
- Products Revenue FY2025: need to check...

Actually, let me also get the products revenue for FY2025 to verify.

Let me calculate:
- FY2025 Consumer Revenue: $7,208 million (disclosed)
- FY2025 Enterprise & Government Revenue: $4,179 million (disclosed)
- Total: $11,387 million ✓

So if Starlink Subscriber Service Revenue = $6,475.8 million, and Consumer Revenue = $7,208 million...

Hmm, that's a bit off. Consumer revenue includes some kit sales too? Or the average subscriber calculation is different from a simple midpoint.

Actually, the subscriber count likely isn't linear throughout the year. A better average might be weighted. But for the purposes of this exercise, the simple midpoint is a reasonable approximation.

Actually wait - let me reconsider. The disclosed Consumer revenue ($7,208M) likely includes both:
1. Consumer Starlink subscriber service revenue
2. Consumer kit product sales

Product revenue for FY2025 would be needed. Let me not go too deep here - I have enough to make a solid analysis.

Let me also verify the FY2025 consumer figure. If we take:
- FY2025 Consumer Revenue: $7,208 million
- FY2025 Product Revenue: let me estimate from Q data... Q1 2025 products = $352M. If I assume roughly similar quarterly products, maybe ~$1,400M annual? But that's a rough guess. Actually the filing might have disclosed annual products.

Let me just present the analysis clearly. The key insight is:

1. **Naive estimate** (total Connectivity revenue / avg subscribers / 3 months) = ~$113/month
2. **Better estimate** (Connectivity service revenue / avg subscribers / 3 months) = ~$100/month  
3. **Disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU** = $66/month

The gap between ~$100 and $66 exists because Connectivity service revenue includes enterprise/government revenue from customers that are NOT counted as Starlink Subscribers.

Let me also verify the simple midpoint assumption is reasonable. The filing says "average number of Starlink Subscribers during the period." Without monthly data, the best approximation is beginning + ending / 2.

OK, I think I have everything I need. Let me compose the answer.
Average subscribers Q1 2026 = 9.6 million. Now let me compute the ARPU calculations.
Now let me back into the implied Starlink subscriber-only revenue using the disclosed ARPU.

So:
- Starlink Subscriber Service Revenue = $1,900.8 million (backed into from disclosed ARPU)
- Enterprise/Other Connectivity Service Revenue = $976.2 million
- Starlink Subscriber revenue = 66.1% of Connectivity service revenue

Now let me also compute Q1 2025 for comparison.
Q1 2025 average subscribers = 4.7 million. Let me compute implied ARPU for Q1 2025 for comparison.

Q1 2025:
- Total Connectivity revenue ARPU: $175.53/month
- Service revenue ARPU: $150.57/month
- Backed-in Starlink Subscriber revenue: $86 × 4.7M × 3 = $1,212.6M
- Enterprise service revenue: $2,123M - $1,212.6M = $910.4M

Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me also calculate the year-over-year change in implied ARPU.

steps.message: Now I have all the data and calculations needed. Let me compile the comprehensive answer.
steps.reasoning_content:
Now I have all the calculations. Let me compile the final answer.

Key findings:

1. **Naive implied ARPU (Total Connectivity Revenue / avg subscribers / 3 months):**
   - Q1 2026: $113.09/month
   - Q1 2025: $175.53/month

2. **Product-adjusted implied ARPU (Connectivity Service Revenue / avg subscribers / 3 months):**
   - Q1 2026: $99.90/month
   - Q1 2025: $150.57/month

3. **Disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU:**
   - Q1 2026: $66/month
   - Q1 2025: $86/month

The gap between the product-adjusted implied ($99.90) and disclosed ($66) is ~51% and represents the enterprise/government revenue in the Connectivity segment that is excluded from the Starlink Subscriber ARPU numerator.

Let me now compile the final answer.

arguments.final_result:
## Implied Monthly Starlink ARPU Estimate — Q1 2026

### Methodology

The filing directly discloses a **Starlink Subscriber ARPU of $66/month** for Q1 2026. However, this is computed from a **narrowly defined** revenue and subscriber base. Below, I reconstruct what a user would arrive at using the broader **Connectivity segment revenue** with the disclosed subscriber counts, identify the resulting overstatement, and enumerate every exclusion.

---

### 1. Data Inputs

| Metric | Q1 2026 | Q1 2025 | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Connectivity Segment Revenue | $3,257M | $2,475M | Segment revenue table |
| Less: Products Revenue (Starlink Kit Sales) | ($380M) | ($352M) | Disaggregated revenue table |
| **Connectivity Service Revenue** | **$2,877M** | **$2,123M** | Calculated |
| Starlink Subscribers (end of period) | 10.3M | 5.0M | Segment data |
| Starlink Subscribers (beginning of period = prior year-end) | 8.9M | 4.4M | Segment data |
| **Average Starlink Subscribers (simple midpoint)** | **9.6M** | **4.7M** | Calculated: (beginning + ending) / 2 |

---

### 2. Implied ARPU Calculations

**Scenario A — Total Connectivity Revenue (incl. products & enterprise/government):**

$$\text{Implied ARPU} = \frac{\$3{,}257\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3\text{ months}} = \$113.09/\text{month}$$

**Scenario B — Connectivity Service Revenue (excluding product/kit sales):**

$$\text{Implied ARPU} = \frac{\$2{,}877\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3\text{ months}} = \$99.90/\text{month}$$

**Scenario C — Disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU (filing's own metric):**

$$\text{Disclosed ARPU} = \$66/\text{month}$$

---

### 3. Reconciliation of the Gap

| Component | Q1 2026 | % of Connectivity Service Revenue |
|---|---|---|
| **Starlink Subscriber Service Revenue** (backed into: $66 × 9.6M × 3) | $1,901M | 66.1% |
| **Enterprise & Government Service Revenue** (residual) | $976M | 33.9% |
| **Total Connectivity Service Revenue** | $2,877M | 100.0% |
| **Product Revenue (Starlink Kit Sales)** | $380M | excluded from ARPU numerator |
| **Total Connectivity Segment Revenue** | $3,257M | — |

The implied ARPU of **$99.90/month** (Scenario B) overstates the filing's disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU of **$66/month** by approximately **$34/month (51%)** because the Connectivity service revenue denominator includes enterprise and government revenue from customers who are **not** counted as Starlink Subscribers.

---

### 4. Assumptions

1. **Average subscriber count = simple midpoint** of beginning-of-period (8.9M at Dec 31, 2025) and end-of-period (10.3M at Mar 31, 2026), yielding 9.6M. The filing states it uses "average number of Starlink Subscribers during the period" but does not disclose monthly granularity; the midpoint is the best available approximation. To the extent subscriber growth was front- or back-loaded within Q1 2026, the true average would differ.

2. **Q1 2026 beginning subscribers = FY2025 ending subscribers (8.9M).** This assumes no restatement or adjustment between the periods.

3. **Product revenue is excluded** from the service-revenue-based ARPU calculation (Scenario B) to better approximate the recurring subscription revenue base. The filing confirms all product revenue is attributable to the Connectivity segment and represents one-time Starlink Kit sales.

---

### 5. What the Filing's Subscriber Count and Revenue Definitions Exclude

The filing's **disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU ($66/month)** differs from a naïve Connectivity revenue-based calculation because of the following exclusions:

#### A. Excluded from the Starlink Subscriber Count (denominator):
- **Managed enterprise customers** with direct, negotiated agreements through the Starlink sales team — including:
  - Aviation customers
  - Maritime customers
  - Land mobility customers
  - Fixed-site enterprise customers
- **Government / Starshield customers** under long-term, multi-year contracts
- These customers are **not** counted even though they consume Connectivity services

The subscriber count reflects only **unique Service Lines** tied to a consumer-facing Starlink.com account, encompassing Personal (Residential, Roam) and Business (Local Priority, Global Priority) plans.

#### B. Excluded from the Starlink Subscriber ARPU Numerator:
- **Enterprise & Government service revenue** ($976M in Q1 2026) — from aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed sites, and Starshield contracts
- **Starlink Mobile (satellite-to-mobile) revenue** — derived from revenue-sharing arrangements with Mobile Network Operator (MNO) partners, covering ~7.4 million monthly unique devices
- **Product revenue** ($380M in Q1 2026) — one-time Starlink Kit hardware sales

The ARPU numerator contains **only** recurring service revenue from the self-service Starlink Subscriber base.

#### C. Additional Metric Nuance:
- A Starlink "Subscriber" = 1 **Service Line**, not 1 person. The filing states the number of individual end users "is already likely meaningfully higher than 10.3 million," as multiple users may share a single Service Line (e.g., a household).

---

### 6. Summary Table

| Measure | Q1 2026 | Q1 2025 | Δ YoY |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Implied ARPU — Total Connectivity Revenue** | **$113.09/mo** | $175.53/mo | -35.6% |
| **Implied ARPU — Connectivity Service Revenue** | **$99.90/mo** | $150.57/mo | -33.6% |
| **Disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU** | **$66.00/mo** | $86.00/mo | -23.3% |
| **Overstatement of naïve vs. disclosed ARPU** | **$33.90/mo (51%)** | $64.57/mo (75%) | — |

The overstatement narrowed from Q1 2025 to Q1 2026, suggesting the Starlink Subscriber base is growing faster than the enterprise/government revenue base, which is consistent with the filing's disclosure that consumer subscriber growth (105% YoY) outpaced enterprise revenue growth.

---

### Sources

- SpaceX S-1 Filing (May 20, 2026): https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm

### Methodology

The filing directly discloses a **Starlink Subscriber ARPU of $66/month** for Q1 2026. However, this is computed from a **narrowly defined** revenue and subscriber base. Below, I reconstruct what a user would arrive at using the broader **Connectivity segment revenue** with the disclosed subscriber counts, identify the resulting overstatement, and enumerate every exclusion.

---

### 1. Data Inputs

| Metric | Q1 2026 | Q1 2025 | Source |

| Connectivity Segment Revenue | $3,257M | $2,475M | Segment revenue table |
| Less: Products Revenue (Starlink Kit Sales) | ($380M) | ($352M) | Disaggregated revenue table |
| **Connectivity Service Revenue** | **$2,877M** | **$2,123M** | Calculated |
| Starlink Subscribers (end of period) | 10.3M | 5.0M | Segment data |
| Starlink Subscribers (beginning of period = prior year-end) | 8.9M | 4.4M | Segment data |
| **Average Starlink Subscribers (simple midpoint)** | **9.6M** | **4.7M** | Calculated: (beginning + ending) / 2 |

---

### 2. Implied ARPU Calculations

**Scenario A — Total Connectivity Revenue (incl. products & enterprise/government):**

$$\text{Implied ARPU} = \frac{\$3{,}257\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3\text{ months}} = \$113.09/\text{month}$$

**Scenario B — Connectivity Service Revenue (excluding product/kit sales):**

$$\text{Implied ARPU} = \frac{\$2{,}877\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3\text{ months}} = \$99.90/\text{month}$$

**Scenario C — Disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU (filing's own metric):**

$$\text{Disclosed ARPU} = \$66/\text{month}$$

---

### 3. Reconciliation of the Gap

| Component | Q1 2026 | % of Connectivity Service Revenue |
|---|---|---|
| **Starlink Subscriber Service Revenue** (backed into: $66 × 9.6M × 3) | $1,901M | 66.1% |
| **Enterprise & Government Service Revenue** (residual) | $976M | 33.9% |
| **Total Connectivity Service Revenue** | $2,877M | 100.0% |
| **Product Revenue (Starlink Kit Sales)** | $380M | excluded from ARPU numerator |
| **Total Connectivity Segment Revenue** | $3,257M | — |

The implied ARPU of **$99.90/month** (Scenario B) overstates the filing's disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU of **$66/month** by approximately **$34/month (51%)** because the Connectivity service revenue denominator includes enterprise and government revenue from customers who are **not** counted as Starlink Subscribers.

---

### 4. Assumptions

1. **Average subscriber count = simple midpoint** of beginning-of-period (8.9M at Dec 31, 2025) and end-of-period (10.3M at Mar 31, 2026), yielding 9.6M. The filing states it uses "average number of Starlink Subscribers during the period" but does not disclose monthly granularity; the midpoint is the best available approximation. To the extent subscriber growth was front- or back-loaded within Q1 2026, the true average would differ.

2. **Q1 2026 beginning subscribers = FY2025 ending subscribers (8.9M).** This assumes no restatement or adjustment between the periods.

3. **Product revenue is excluded** from the service-revenue-based ARPU calculation (Scenario B) to better approximate the recurring subscription revenue base. The filing confirms all product revenue is attributable to the Connectivity segment and represents one-time Starlink Kit sales.

---

### 5. What the Filing's Subscriber Count and Revenue Definitions Exclude

The filing's **disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU ($66/month)** differs from a naïve Connectivity revenue-based calculation because of the following exclusions:

#### A. Excluded from the Starlink Subscriber Count (denominator):
- **Managed enterprise customers** with direct, negotiated agreements through the Starlink sales team — including:
  - Aviation customers
  - Maritime customers
  - Land mobility customers
  - Fixed-site enterprise customers
- **Government / Starshield customers** under long-term, multi-year contracts
- These customers are **not** counted even though they consume Connectivity services

The subscriber count reflects only **unique Service Lines** tied to a consumer-facing Starlink.com account, encompassing Personal (Residential, Roam) and Business (Local Priority, Global Priority) plans.

#### B. Excluded from the Starlink Subscriber ARPU Numerator:
- **Enterprise & Government service revenue** ($976M in Q1 2026) — from aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed sites, and Starshield contracts
- **Starlink Mobile (satellite-to-mobile) revenue** — derived from revenue-sharing arrangements with Mobile Network Operator (MNO) partners, covering ~7.4 million monthly unique devices
- **Product revenue** ($380M in Q1 2026) — one-time Starlink Kit hardware sales

The ARPU numerator contains **only** recurring service revenue from the self-service Starlink Subscriber base.

#### C. Additional Metric Nuance:
- A Starlink "Subscriber" = 1 **Service Line**, not 1 person. The filing states the number of individual end users "is already likely meaningfully higher than 10.3 million," as multiple users may share a single Service Line (e.g., a household).

---

### 6. Summary Table

| Measure | Q1 2026 | Q1 2025 | Δ YoY |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Implied ARPU — Total Connectivity Revenue** | **$113.09/mo** | $175.53/mo | -35.6% |
| **Implied ARPU — Connectivity Service Revenue** | **$99.90/mo** | $150.57/mo | -33.6% |
| **Disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU** | **$66.00/mo** | $86.00/mo | -23.3% |
| **Overstatement of naïve vs. disclosed ARPU** | **$33.90/mo (51%)** | $64.57/mo (75%) | — |
